what_it_does: This file is a YAML-frontmatter ledger record that registers an image attachment (att-1e6bf204) in the design-source channel, pointing to the image asset image://design-source/presentation-15p/p-06 and recording when it was added.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content
Attachment id att-1e6bf204 with channel set to design-source
No code symbols or prose description
standouts: Opaque image:// URI target pointing to presentation-15p/p-06
Attachment has its own first-class id separate from the target asset
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Id prefix att- for attachment records
Target asset referenced by a channel-scoped URI
Minimal, metadata-only file with no narrative content
concerns: Target URI gives no human-readable description of the image
No provenance fields such as author or editor
No checksum, dimensions, or alt text for the referenced image
attachment_type value images is ambiguous about whether it refers to one image or a collection
No explicit link to the parent channel-memory entry it attaches to
notes: A bare attachment ledger stub that maps a design-source image asset URI to a unique attachment id and an addition timestamp.
questions: What does the image at presentation-15p/p-06 actually depict?
Which channel-memory entry is this attachment associated with?
Was the image added by a person or generated by a process?
Does the referenced image:// asset currently exist and is it versioned?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears intended to catalog an image attachment; it does exactly that, albeit with very sparse metadata.
apparent_themes: Separation of attachment metadata from the asset itself
intention_signals: It is trying to create a durable, queryable ledger entry that links a design-source channel memory to a specific image slide or asset without embedding the image.
novelty: The use of an image:// URI scheme as the target and the treatment of an attachment as a first-class ledger object with its own id.
contribution: It contributes a canonical attachment reference consisting of a stable id, channel, attachment type, target URI, and addition timestamp that a fused system could use to locate and contextualize the referenced image asset.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML metadata attachment record in the design-source channel that references the image asset image://design-source/presentation-15p/p-06 under id att-1e6bf204 with an addition timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To register and timestamp an image attachment tied to a design-source channel memory item.
